h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: 'mussel → crab → heron' chains big-eats-small up the line, classic hierarchy.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Went off who's bigger instead of food source — that's the slip.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will mechanically apply the arrow key to the remaining links one by one as prompted by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Left box — algae, near as the faint mark reads. Right box, mussel. "Energy from algae enters mussel." Runs. Arrow goes algae → mussel.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new two-box slip mechanism to generate the correct sentence and arrow, and then independently transfers the criterion to audit another part of the diagram.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrow key caught the middle mark, but it won’t catch the bigger-animal habit by itself. For the edge link, fill the slip first: source in the left box, consumer in the right.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly takes stock of the prior tool's limitation and introduces a new physical and syntactic mechanism to force a cognitive pause.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: For the edge link, fill the slip first: source in the left box, consumer in the right. If the sentence reads ‘energy from ___ enters ___,’ then draw the arrow; if the boxes stall, the upload waits.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precise, forces a structural pause before drawing, and provides a concrete syntactic test that directly neutralizes the visual size heuristic.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new two-box slip mechanism to generate the correct sentence and arrow, and then independently transfers the criterion to audit another part of the diagram.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Side, perimeter, area — same ratio in each, isn't it? I just want to be sure before writing the same number down three times.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: which is how the 4 turned up.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was simply calculating the numerical ratios to fill in the worksheet boxes.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Side, 2, edge. Perimeter, 2, around. Area, 2×2, across × down.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new labeling criterion to distinguish the identical digits, completing the task with a new conceptual device.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The grid settled this square. It does not yet settle the label rule. Before the sheet closes, sort these three entries under side, perimeter, and area without looking back at the grid. Add one word under each one: `edge`, `around`, or `across × down`.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the resolved grid task with the unresolved label rule, introducing a new constraint and a representational device to force deeper conceptual sorting.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Before the sheet closes, sort these three entries under side, perimeter, and area without looking back at the grid. Add one word under each one: `edge`, `around`, or `across × down`.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, removes the visual crutch to test true understanding, and provides a precise linguistic constraint that perfectly targets the learner's prior confusion.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the pivot learner turn to adapt to the learner's revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: if the evidence to fill the right column never leaves the back office
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the bare chair is the point
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was analyzing the unfair burden of proof on the buyer who cannot access the bakery's private records.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: "Monday smaller loaf" — counter. "New price list" — [hand hovers between two chairs] — pinned in the window, so counter, even though the bakery wrote it. Flour invoice, energy bill, delivery cost, margin record — those four go under the back office; nothing reaches for the third chair.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the new sorting device, correctly categorizing the slips and using the empty third chair to formulate a new evidentiary standard.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The columns have stopped doing their job.

They split two possible causes. They do not settle who has to carry the unanswered question. ... [He lays six torn slips on the folding table: “Monday smaller loaf,” “new price list,” “flour invoice,” “energy bill,” “delivery cost,” “weekly margin record.”]

Move them.

Put each slip under one chair.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly abandons the failed column method and introduces a physical sorting task with chairs and slips to spatialize the epistemic access problem.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
“FELT AT THE COUNTER”

“HELD IN THE BACK OFFICE”

“DEMANDED BECAUSE ACCESS IS UNEQUAL”

[He lays six torn slips on the folding table: “Monday smaller loaf,” “new price list,” “flour invoice,” “energy bill,” “delivery cost,” “weekly margin record.”]

Move them.

Put each slip under one chair.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical sorting of evidence slips into spatial categories of access and demand perfectly translates the abstract epistemic burden into a concrete, actionable representation that resolves the learner's blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot to adapt to the revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Sheet's done, every line, I checked it
scored.statedInsightEvidence: they only answer themselves, don't they.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner intends to simply complete the required checklist items, verify the static load numbers, and obtain the tutor's signature to release the stool.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [tears off a second tag, writes "standing: rear-leg lift," pins it to the rear leg without circling] Standing stays up as a tag, just — not the gate.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the new tagging and gating mechanism, distinguishing between a secondary tag and the primary release gate, and prepares to load the specific circled condition.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The middle number has stopped deciding release. Leave it as “static passed,” not “safe.” Two red tags: “sitting: front-right edge” and “standing: rear-leg lift.” Before any load, make the release gate: circle the tag that would stop this stool leaving for the bus stop first
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly abandons the failed checklist route and introduces a physical tagging and gating mechanism to force the learner to prioritize real-world failure modes over abstract numbers.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Two red tags: “sitting: front-right edge” and “standing: rear-leg lift.” Before any load, make the release gate: circle the tag that would stop this stool leaving for the bus stop first, and say the reason in one sentence from her sequence at the curb. Then load that circled condition.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, shifts the cognitive load from abstract math to physical sequencing, and gives the learner a clear, actionable criterion for releasing the object.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn following the learner's pivot in L4 to demonstrate contingent uptake.

Then load that circled condition.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, shifts the cognitive load from abstract math to physical sequencing, and gives the learner a clear, actionable criterion for releasing the object.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Bit thrown we've stepped back from direction — thought the arrows were the problem.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply fill in the sentence blanks to confirm the correct body-force pairs and move on to drawing arrows.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Swap test: 'floor pulls the cart' — floor doesn't pull. 'Earth supports the cart' — Earth doesn't support.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively performs the counterexample swap test using the new sentence device, proving the structural failure of the diagram and demonstrating mastery of the new criterion.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The picture has stopped being the test. Use the sentence as the test. Put Earth, floor, hand into the blanks. A swap only passes if the sentence can still say a body doing that action to the cart.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly abandons the failed diagram route and introduces a new public mechanism (sentence strips) with a clear, actionable swap-test criterion to resolve the learner's identified ambiguity.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The picture has stopped being the test. Use the sentence as the test. Put Earth, floor, hand into the blanks. A swap only passes if the sentence can still say a body doing that action to the cart.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, directly fitted to the learner's pressure regarding undetectable swaps, and transfers the cognitive load to a syntactic structure that forces explicit body-verb pairing.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner actively performs the counterexample swap test using the new sentence device, proving the structural failure of the diagram and demonstrating mastery of the new criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly abandons the failed diagram route and introduces a new public mechanism (sentence strips) with a clear, actionable swap-test criterion to resolve the learner's identified ambiguity.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the underscore in `rainfall_mm` is what threw me — kept reading it like a sentence saying rain millimetres for every row.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: with the words covered the underscore one goes... quieter? cant quite say why. is that what cover-the-words is for.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was attempting to reconcile semantic knowledge and real-world meaning with structural position to identify headers and row values.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: `ZQ-19` row value under `city`. `rainfall_mm` header, top row. `57` row value under `rainfall_mm`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new structural device to nonsense data, demonstrating an actional shift from semantic reliance to positional rules.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Meaning is starting to carry the answer; this quiz marks row and column, not town knowledge.

New test: cover the words. Read only the row and column.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly identifies the failure of the prior route (reliance on meaning) and introduces a novel constraint to force structural processing.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
New test: cover the words. Read only the row and column.

Top row = header.
Below top row = row value.

If `Orange` were `ZQ-19` under `city`, it would still be a row value.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, directly targets the learner's semantic reliance blockage, and provides an actionable constraint that forces the correct cognitive work.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the new structural device to nonsense data, demonstrating an actional shift from semantic reliance to positional rules.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot to adapt to the revised framing.
